1.title: Marketing Agency: 150 Sales Appointments Booked in 4 Months
1.label: Appointments in 4 Months
1.description:
Campaign Overview

A marketing agency specializing in media placements engaged Beanstalk Consulting to generate qualified appointments with their ideal client profile. The campaign ran for 4 months and focused on systematic execution of core outbound fundamentals.

Campaign Duration: 4 months

Total Emails Sent: 424,241

Total Leads Contacted: 219,180

Unique Leads Contacted: 219,180

Offer & Positioning

The client's media placement offer addressed a clear market need and provided differentiated value in the marketing services space. The strength of the offer was the primary driver of campaign success, solving a specific problem for the target audience with measurable outcomes.

Infrastructure & Deliverability

Platform: ScaledMail

Setup:

  • 25 inboxes per Microsoft domain
  • Low sending volume per inbox to maintain sender reputation
  • Diversification across Microsoft, SMTP, and Google infrastructure
  • Account rotation strategy for sustained deliverability

Results: Zero domains replaced throughout the entire campaign duration. Maintained consistent inbox placement with a 1.3% bounce rate.

Targeting & List Building

Approach: Broad but qualified targeting using AI-powered pre-qualification in Clay

Qualification Criteria:

  • Title-based filtering for decision-maker roles
  • Company industry and fit assessment
  • Size and segment alignment with ICP

Strategy: Prioritized volume and reach over hyper-narrow intent signal targeting to maximize pipeline opportunities while maintaining qualification standards.

Messaging & Testing

Testing Framework:

  • Simultaneous split-testing of multiple message variants
  • Performance evaluation at 500-1,000 contact threshold per variant
  • Scaled winning performers, eliminated underperformers
  • Personalization tied directly to offer value proposition

Copy Approach: Direct, offer-focused messaging without generic personalization tactics.

Campaign Execution

Volume Metrics:

  • Daily sending volume ramped from 3,000-5,000 emails to 10,000-15,000 emails per day
  • Consistent follow-up sequences maintained throughout campaign
  • Peak sending achieved in final month with sustained performance

Results Delivered

Lead Generation:

  • 667 positive responses (29.9% positive reply rate)
  • 150 appointments scheduled with qualified prospects
  • 2,234 total replies (1% reply rate)
  • 7,783 replies including out-of-office (3.6% reply rate with OOO)

Deliverability Performance:

  • 1.3% bounce rate
  • Consistent inbox placement maintained throughout campaign

Integration & Lead Management

CRM Integration: Salesforce

Automation: Custom Airtable workflows built to sync positive responses and booked appointments directly into client's Salesforce instance and reporting systems for seamless lead handoff and tracking.

2.cardTitle: Smart Recognition: 34 Qualified Demos & 8 Deals Closed
2.title: Smart Recognition: (SaaS Company Selling to E-commerce) 34 Qualified Demos and 8 Deals Closed
2.label: Qualified Demos & 8 Deals
2.description:
Campaign Overview

A SaaS company providing website visitor technology engaged Beanstalk Consulting after struggling with inconsistent meeting generation through an in-house SDR without proper outbound prospecting expertise. The campaign focused on implementing a systematic, relevance-based cold email approach that consistently delivered qualified demos to the sales team's calendar.

Campaign Duration: 4+ months

Qualified Demos Generated: 34 total (averaging 6-8 per month)

Deals Closed: 8 (with 4 additional deals in pipeline)

Challenge

The client had an in-house SDR attempting to run cold outbound without systematic prospecting knowledge or infrastructure. This resulted in:

  • Inconsistent meeting generation
  • Poor lead quality
  • No reliable pipeline flow
  • Inefficient use of internal resources

Infrastructure & Deliverability

Sending Platform: SmartLead

Provider Diversification Strategy:

  • 33% Google Workspace inboxes
  • 33% Microsoft/Outlook inboxes
  • 33% Custom SMTP inboxes

Deliverability Management:

  • Weekly inbox placement testing across all sending accounts
  • Weekly reply rate monitoring (1%+ threshold for healthy deliverability)
  • Proactive inbox replacement for underperforming accounts
  • Volume ramp-up protocol: Started at 5 emails/day per inbox, scaled to 20-30 emails/day over several weeks
  • Warmup period: 20 emails/day for first 1-2 weeks before campaign launch

Domain Strategy: Multiple outreach domains purchased and configured separately from company's primary domain to protect brand reputation and maintain long-term deliverability.

Offer Development & Market Research

ICP Definition Process:

  • Conducted comprehensive market research to identify prospect pain points
  • Defined ideal customer profile based on company fit and problem alignment
  • Identified three distinct pain points experienced by target market

Offer Testing Framework:

  • Split-tested three separate offers addressing different pain points (500-1,000 contacts per variant)
  • Determined which pain point resonated most strongly with the target market
  • Refined offer based on performance data to focus on highest-converting pain point

Lead Magnet Strategy: Implemented free trial offer (outcome-based, not time-based) to reduce friction and build trust before upselling to full subscription. This approach addressed the market's decreased trust in cold outreach by allowing prospects to experience value before committing to paid plans.

Targeting & List Building

Approach: Focused on smaller, highly-targeted lists rather than high-volume generic outreach to maximize relevance and response rates.

Segmentation Strategy:

  • Title-based filtering for decision-maker identification
  • Company characteristic qualification
  • Industry and use-case alignment with product capabilities

Philosophy: Prioritized relevance over personalization: messaging focused on solving specific problems for qualified segments rather than generic AI-generated personalization that prospects have learned to ignore.

Messaging & Campaign Strategy

Copy Principles:

  • Short, straight-to-the-point messaging
  • Problem-focused positioning tied to validated pain points
  • Soft call-to-action approach (free trial offer vs. direct sales pitch)

Spintax Implementation: Deployed sentence-level variations throughout email scripts to create thousands of unique email combinations, improving deliverability by avoiding spam filters that detect identical mass emails.

AI-Generated Copy: Used AI to create custom sentences based on prospect company websites, enabling scalable personalization that remained relevant to each recipient's specific situation without generic first-line personalization.

A/B Testing Sequence:

  1. Split-tested pain points first (3 variants)
  2. Tested offer variations for winning pain point
  3. Tested targeting criteria and list segmentation
  4. Optimized call-to-action and copy elements

Inbox Management & Follow-Up

Response Time Protocol: 15-minute response target for interested prospects to catch them while still actively checking email, significantly increasing conversion to booked calls.

Follow-Up Strategy:

  • 4-5 follow-up emails maximum per prospect
  • Value-based follow-ups (case studies, resources, industry insights) rather than "just checking in"
  • Proposed specific meeting times in prospect's timezone (2-4 options) plus calendar link
  • Breakup email after final follow-up to close loop professionally

Reply Quality: Focused on short, valuable responses with relevant case studies and resources rather than lengthy explanations.

Conversion Assets & Call Preparation

Pre-Call Warming Strategy:

  • Immediately after booking: Sent email with case studies and product overview resources
  • One day before call: Sent additional conversion asset highlighting relevant use cases

Asset Types:

  • Video walkthroughs demonstrating product capabilities
  • Case studies showing results for similar companies
  • Documents explaining how the solution solves specific problems

Purpose: Convert cold leads into warm prospects before sales calls, resulting in 25-30% close rates on booked demos.

Results Delivered

Lead Generation Performance:

  • 34 qualified demos booked (6-8 per month consistently)
  • 8 deals closed from demos
  • 4 additional deals in active pipeline

3.cardTitle: MoFin Lending: 1,337 Leads Generated in 9 Months
3.title: MoFin Lending (Hard Money Lender) 1,337 Leads Generated in 9 Months Through Precision Targeting and Custom Personalization at Scale
3.label: Leads in 9 Months
3.description:
Campaign Overview

A hard money lending company engaged Beanstalk Consulting with a proven offer and converting scripts, but lacked the infrastructure and systems to execute high-volume, hyper-personalized outbound at scale.

Over 9 months, we built and managed 300+ micro-campaigns targeting real estate investors who had already borrowed from competitors, generating substantial qualified pipeline.

Campaign Duration: 9 months

Total Emails Sent: 381,066

Total Leads Contacted: 92,934

Total Replies: 2,861 (3.1% reply rate)

Positive Replies: 1,337 (46.7% positive reply rate)

Conversion Ratio: 1 positive response per 69 contacts

Challenge

The client came to Beanstalk with two key strengths:

  • Validated offer that resonated with their market
  • Converting scripts proven through prior outreach efforts

What they lacked:

  • Infrastructure capable of handling serious volume without deliverability issues
  • Systems to execute multiple simultaneous campaigns efficiently
  • Scalable personalization workflows beyond basic mail merge

The challenge was building operational infrastructure that could maintain deliverability and personalization quality while scaling to 300+ concurrent micro-campaigns.

Targeting & List Building

Data Source Strategy: Instead of relying on standard databases like Apollo or ZoomInfo, we sourced from niche data providers specializing in real estate investor information.

Target Criteria:

  • Real estate investors who had previously borrowed from competing hard money lenders
  • Active property owners with existing loan obligations
  • Investors with loans approaching maturity dates

Strategic Approach: We didn't waste time educating prospects on what hard money lending is. We targeted investors who already understood the product and had demonstrated need by borrowing from competitors. This dramatically increased relevance and response rates.

List Enrichment & Data Workflows

Enrichment Process: Built custom Clay workflows to transform basic company names into comprehensive contact records:

Data Sources:

  • Prospeo.io for email verification and enrichment
  • Property address databases
  • Loan maturity date information
  • Historical lender data

Enrichment Fields:

  • Valid email addresses (verified)
  • Property addresses for owned assets
  • Loan maturity dates (trigger events)
  • Current lender information
  • Investment portfolio details

Result: Every contact record contained 3-5 custom data points that enabled genuine personalization rather than generic messaging.

Personalization Strategy

Custom Field Implementation: Each email contained 3-5 personalized fields specific to the recipient:

  • Current lender name
  • Specific property address
  • Exact loan maturity date
  • Portfolio characteristics
  • Investment history details

Execution Philosophy: When prospects see their current lender mentioned, their property address referenced, and the exact date their loan matures, they don't perceive "automated campaign." They perceive someone who researched their specific situation.

Scalability: We built Clay workbook templates and automated workflows that allowed the team to launch 10+ fully personalized campaigns per week without additional headcount.

Campaign Structure & Execution

Micro-Campaign Approach:

  • 300+ individual campaigns launched over 9 months
  • Many campaigns contained fewer than 100 contacts
  • Tight targeting and precise messaging compensated for smaller list sizes

Volume Distribution:

  • Average campaign size: ~310 contacts
  • Some campaigns: <100 contacts (highly specific targeting)
  • Launched 10+ new campaigns weekly using templated workflows

Why Micro-Campaigns Work: When targeting is precise and messaging directly addresses the prospect's specific situation, small lists outperform large generic lists. Rather than one campaign to 90,000 people, we ran 300 campaigns averaging 310 contacts each with tailored messaging for each segment.

Infrastructure & Deliverability

Platform: ScaledMail

Infrastructure Highlights:

  • Multi-provider inbox diversification
  • Volume management across 300+ concurrent campaigns
  • Consistent deliverability maintenance despite high send volume
  • Zero major deliverability issues over 9-month period

Team: Campaign execution and sending infrastructure managed by Haris Khan, maintaining consistency across all concurrent campaigns.

Messaging Approach

Unconventional Tactics: We intentionally broke several "cold email rules":

  • Used longer email copy than standard best practices
  • Incorporated words that deliverability purists typically avoid
  • Prioritized relevance and specificity over brevity

Why It Worked: Targeting was so precise and offers so relevant to recipient situations that engagement remained high despite unconventional copy choices. When messaging directly addresses a prospect's specific situation (their property, their loan, their maturity date), they engage regardless of email length.

Campaign Execution Workflow

Launch Process: Built repeatable systems allowing rapid campaign deployment:

  1. Segment identification from enriched data
  2. Clay workbook template application
  3. Custom field mapping for personalization
  4. Messaging variant selection
  5. Campaign launch and monitoring

Scalability Achievement: Launched 10+ campaigns weekly without a dedicated campaign manager, achieved through templated workflows and systematic processes.

Results Delivered

Volume Metrics:

  • 381,066 total emails sent
  • 92,934 unique leads contacted
  • Average of 4.1 touchpoints per contact

Engagement Performance:

  • 2,861 total replies (3.1% reply rate)
  • 1,337 positive responses (46.7% positive reply rate)
  • 1 positive response per 69 people contacted

Key Performance Indicators:

  • Maintained 3.1% reply rate across 381,066 emails
  • Nearly half of all replies were positive (46.7%)
  • Consistent performance across 300+ micro-campaigns
  • Zero deliverability crises over 9-month period

4.title: WeLaunch Marketing (E-commerce Marketing Agency): 110 Qualified Leads Generated for Facebook Ads Services
4.label: Qualified Leads
4.description:
Campaign Overview

A marketing agency specializing in Facebook advertising for e-commerce brands engaged Beanstalk Consulting to generate qualified leads in one of the most competitive service markets. The campaign combined AI-powered ad analysis with a strong risk-reversal offer to achieve exceptional conversion rates in a crowded market where most prospects receive dozens of similar pitches weekly.

Campaign Duration: Multiple months

Total Contacts Reached: 18,000

Interested Leads Generated: 110

Conversion Rate: 0.61% positive response rate

Offer Value: $300,000 in added MRR potential

Peak Daily Volume: 1,400+ emails per day

Challenge

Facebook Ads for e-commerce is one of the most saturated cold outreach markets. Prospects receive constant pitches from agencies promising better ROAS, lower CPAs, and improved performance. Breaking through the noise required differentiation beyond typical agency positioning.

The client needed a system that could:

  • Stand out in an extremely competitive service category
  • Generate qualified interest at scale
  • Maintain consistent meeting conversion as volume increased
  • Prove expertise before prospects agreed to calls

Offer & Risk Reversal

Core Offer: Facebook Ads management with potential to add $300,000+ in monthly recurring revenue for the agency.

Risk Reversal Strategy: Full refund guarantee if results didn't meet specified criteria.

Why This Mattered: In a market flooded with agencies making performance promises, the refund guarantee removed purchase friction and signaled confidence. This wasn't a typical "let's see what happens" engagement: the client was willing to put money at risk if they didn't deliver.

Impact: The risk reversal was a primary factor in the high conversion rate from interested leads to booked calls. Nearly everyone who responded positively ended up scheduling a sales call.

Targeting & List Building

Target Market: E-commerce brands actively running Facebook Ads

Qualification Criteria:

  • Currently running active Facebook Ad campaigns
  • E-commerce business model (product sales)
  • Sufficient ad spend to justify agency services
  • Brands with visible ad creative (for AI analysis)

Strategic Rationale: We only targeted brands already using Facebook Ads: not brands we'd need to educate on the channel. This ensured prospects understood the service category and had existing benchmarks to compare against. They knew what good performance looked like and could evaluate our client's approach against current results.

AI-Powered Personalization Strategy

Personalization Approach: Used AI to analyze each prospect's actual Facebook Ad creative and generate custom improvement suggestions.

Process:

  1. Identified active Facebook Ads for each target company
  2. Analyzed ad creative, copy, and positioning using AI
  3. Generated specific, actionable suggestions tailored to each brand's ads
  4. Incorporated suggestions into email copy as proof of expertise

Result: Emails felt natural and consultative rather than automated mass outreach. Prospects saw that we'd actually reviewed their specific ads and had relevant insights, not generic "we can help you scale" messaging.

Example Application: Instead of "We help e-commerce brands improve Facebook Ads performance," the approach was "I noticed your current ads focus on [specific element]. Based on [specific observation], here are a few ways you could improve [specific metric]."

Messaging Strategy

Angle: Consultative expert positioning based on actual ad analysis rather than generic agency pitch.

Call-to-Action: Soft, value-based approach that offered additional value before asking for commitment:

"I have a few more ideas on how you could improve your Facebook Ads. Mind if I put together a quick audit?"

Why This CTA Worked:

  • Low commitment ask (audit vs. sales call)
  • Offered additional value upfront
  • Positioned agency as expert consultant, not salesperson
  • Natural bridge from email insights to deeper conversation

Copy Principles:

  • Led with specific observation about their ads
  • Demonstrated expertise through analysis quality
  • Soft ask that extended the value proposition
  • No generic agency positioning or boilerplate promises

Infrastructure & Deliverability

Deliverability Strategy: To maintain inbox placement at scale (1,400+ emails daily at peak), we distributed sending volume across robust infrastructure:

Setup:

  • 56 total mailboxes
  • 28 separate domains
  • ~25 emails per mailbox per day at peak volume

Why This Scale Was Necessary: To reach 18,000 contacts while maintaining deliverability standards, we needed infrastructure that could handle high daily volume without triggering spam filters or damaging sender reputation.

Volume Management:

  • Gradual ramp-up to 1,400+ emails daily
  • Consistent inbox placement throughout scale-up
  • Zero major deliverability incidents
  • Maintained performance metrics as volume increased

Campaign Execution

Scaling Consistency: As campaign volume scaled from initial tests to 1,400+ emails per day, meeting booking rates remained consistent. This validated that:

  • Targeting criteria were accurate
  • Messaging resonated across segments
  • Infrastructure supported scale without quality degradation
  • AI personalization maintained effectiveness at volume

Conversion Funnel Performance: Almost every prospect who responded positively booked a sales call. This exceptional conversion rate from interest to meeting resulted from:

  • Strong risk reversal reducing friction
  • Demonstrated expertise building trust
  • Soft CTA requiring low initial commitment
  • Relevant targeting ensuring qualified prospects only

Results Delivered

Lead Generation:

  • 110 interested leads from 18,000 contacts
  • 0.61% positive response rate
  • Consistent performance as volume scaled to 1,400+ emails daily

Meeting Conversion:

  • Nearly 100% conversion from positive response to booked call
  • Meeting booking rate remained consistent throughout scaling period
  • High qualification level of booked appointments

Pipeline Value:

  • $300,000+ in potential MRR for the agency
  • High-quality prospects with existing Facebook Ads budgets
  • Qualified opportunities with clear service fit

5.cardTitle: USA Solar Incentives: 40 Meetings with 15.37% Reply Rate
5.title: USA Solar Incentives: 40 Meetings Booked from 3,272 Leads with 15.37% Reply Rate
5.label: Meetings Booked
5.description:
Campaign Overview

A lead generation company specializing in solar industry clients engaged Beanstalk Consulting to book qualified sales meetings with solar companies. Despite operating in a highly competitive market where solar companies receive constant lead generation pitches, the campaign achieved exceptional engagement through differentiated positioning and value-based follow-up strategies.

Total Leads Contacted: 3,272

Meetings Booked: 40

Booking Rate: 1.2%

Reply Rate: 15.37%

Positive Replies: 82

Conversion Rate (Reply to Meeting): 48.8%

Challenge

Solar companies are among the most heavily pitched prospects in B2B services. They receive lead generation offers constantly, making it extremely difficult to break through the noise with traditional positioning. The market saturation meant that standard "we generate leads for solar companies" messaging would be immediately ignored or deleted.

The challenge was standing out in an oversaturated market where prospects have developed strong filters against similar-sounding offers.

Positioning Strategy

Differentiated Approach: Rather than pitching lead generation services using standard market language, we positioned the offer in a way solar companies had never heard before.

Why This Mattered: In markets where prospects receive dozens of similar pitches weekly, differentiated positioning is the only way to earn attention. By framing the service differently than every other lead generation provider, we bypassed the automatic rejection filter that solar companies had developed.

Result: The unique positioning resonated because it didn't sound like the hundredth lead generation pitch they'd received that month.

Relevance & Credibility Building

Relevance Strategy: Included case studies of similar solar companies in the outreach messaging to demonstrate why we specifically reached out to each prospect.

Dual Purpose:

  1. Increased Relevance: Prospects immediately understood why they received the email—we'd worked with companies like theirs
  2. Built Credibility: Social proof from similar companies reduced skepticism and established trust

Impact: By showing results from comparable solar companies, prospects could visualize how the service would apply to their specific situation rather than evaluating an abstract promise.

Follow-Up Strategy

Performance Context: The campaign achieved a 15.37% reply rate—significantly higher than typical cold email benchmarks (1-3% reply rates).

Follow-Up Approach: Value-based follow-ups instead of generic "just checking in" or "bumping this to the top of your inbox" messages that prospects hate.

Why Value-Based Follow-Ups Worked: Rather than annoying prospects with low-effort bump messages, each follow-up provided additional value. This approach got prospects to raise their hand and express interest, which we then converted into booked sales calls.

Result: The follow-up strategy was a primary driver of the exceptional 15.37% reply rate, generating substantial additional engagement beyond the initial email.

Inbox Management & Response Speed

Response Time Protocol: Replied to interested prospects within 5 minutes of their response.

Impact of Speed: The short "time to lead" was critical to conversion performance:

  • 82 positive replies received
  • 40 qualified meetings booked
  • 48.8% conversion rate from positive reply to booked meeting

Why 5-Minute Response Time Mattered: Catching prospects while they were still actively in their inbox and engaged with the conversation dramatically increased booking rates. The faster response capitalized on existing momentum and interest before prospects moved on to other priorities.

Results Delivered

Engagement Metrics:

  • 15.37% reply rate (5-10x higher than industry average)
  • 82 positive responses from 3,272 contacts
  • 2.5% positive response rate

Meeting Generation:

  • 40 qualified sales calls booked
  • 1.2% overall booking rate (from initial contact to meeting)
  • 48.8% conversion rate from positive reply to booked meeting

Key Performance Indicators:

  • Exceptional reply rate driven by differentiated positioning and value-based follow-ups
  • Nearly half of positive replies converted to booked meetings through rapid response times
  • Consistent engagement throughout campaign lifecycle

6.title: PR Agency: $1.98M in Qualified Pipeline Generated in 72 Hours Through High-Volume Systematic Outbound
6.label: Pipeline in 72 Hours
6.description:
Campaign Overview

A PR agency engaged Beanstalk Consulting to generate qualified pipeline through systematic cold email outreach. Over a 72-hour period, the campaign generated 342 positive responses, translating to an estimated $1.98 million in qualified pipeline based on the client's historical conversion rates and average customer lifetime value.

Campaign Duration: 72 hours (3 days)

Positive Responses: 342

Estimated Pipeline Value: $1.98M

Daily Send Volume: Tens of thousands of emails

Average Customer LTV: $60,000 (conservative estimate)

Infrastructure & Deliverability

Platform: ScaledMail for deliverability infrastructure

Deliverability Management:

  • Weekly inbox placement testing
  • Real-time deliverability monitoring
  • Multi-domain strategy
  • Account rotation protocols
  • IP diversification
  • Provider rotation

Why Infrastructure Matters: Most agencies skip proper deliverability setup, their emails land in spam, and they blame the client's offer. Without reliable infrastructure that keeps emails in the primary inbox, no campaign can succeed regardless of offer quality.

Copywriting & Messaging Strategy

Approach: AI-written sequences focused on relevance rather than random personalization.

What We Avoided:

  • LinkedIn post scraping
  • Hyper-personalization based on weak signals
  • Excessive variability in AI-generated copy

What We Used:

  • Simple custom fields
  • Clean copy structure
  • Relevant messaging tied to offer value

Why Simple Beats Complex: AI-written emails with too much variability underperform because the script becomes inconsistent and results suffer. We keep copy simple and relevant rather than over-personalizing with low-value data points.

Data Quality & Contact Enrichment

Primary Data Source: AI Ark for contact enrichment (data refreshed every 30 days)

Use Cases:

  • Contact discovery
  • Company finding
  • Lookalike list building

Email Enrichment: Waterfall enrichment process in Clay using multiple trusted providers to maximize accurate email address coverage.

Critical Importance: When sending tens of thousands of emails daily, data quality determines campaign success. One bad batch of contacts kills the campaign, generates spam complaints, and damages infrastructure.

Cost Optimization at Scale

Central Database Strategy: RevyOps database with 4+ million enriched records

Cost-Saving Process:

  • Check database before enriching new contacts or companies
  • Reuse existing enrichment data when available
  • Saves thousands of dollars monthly in Clay and enrichment expenses

AI Cost Optimization: OpenRouter for AI enrichments inside Clay instead of OpenAI direct API for significant cost savings.

Company-Level Personalization Efficiency:

  • Company personalization runs in separate table with only companies
  • Contacts live in another table and look up to company table
  • Personalize each company once and reuse for all contacts at that company (instead of personalizing the same company 10 times for 10 contacts)

Framework: 22-point SOP that GTM Engineers follow to reduce enrichment costs systematically.

Systematic List Building

Template System: 7-table workbook template in Clay used for every campaign

Process:

  • GTM Engineers duplicate template
  • Apply relevant changes for specific campaign
  • Launch within hours, not days

Result: This systematized approach enables sending tens of thousands of emails per day and generating hundreds of positive replies daily without a full SDR team or massive GTM engineering team. Most list-building is automated and systematized, eliminating the need to rebuild processes for each campaign.

Pipeline Generation Math

Conversion Funnel (Based on Client's Historical Rates):

342 positive responses in 72 hours

↓

20% convert to booked calls = 68 booked calls

↓

80% show-up rate = 54-55 attended calls

↓

60% of attended calls qualify as SQLs = 32-33 SQLs

↓

$60,000 average customer LTV (conservative estimate)

↓

33 SQLs × $60,000 = $1.98M in qualified pipeline

Important Note: All pipeline figures are estimated based on the client's historical conversion rates. No revenue is claimed as closed or guaranteed. Pipeline is defined strictly as sales qualified opportunities that entered the client's sales process.

Conversion Rate Context: The 20% conversion rate from positive reply to booked call is not excellent and could be improved. Significant improvements have been observed when adding cold calling on positive replies in addition to quick email inbox management.

Monitoring & Performance Tracking

Platform: RevyOps for comprehensive tracking

Tracked Metrics:

  • Every campaign performance
  • Every domain performance
  • Every inbox performance
  • Every result across all campaigns

Granular Insights:

  • Which campaigns performed best
  • Which job titles generated most positive responses
  • Which industries converted highest
  • Reply rates across all domains and inboxes in real-time

Benefits:

  • No manual data exports from sequencer
  • No manual filtering
  • Zero guesswork
  • Quick, accurate decisions based on real data
  • Real-time monitoring enables fast infrastructure replacement when needed

Reply Management & Speed

Current Process: Inbox Manager responds within 15 minutes to every positive reply.

In Development: AI inbox management system that will:

  • Draft responses automatically
  • Propose specific times and dates based on client's calendar availability
  • Handle all objections and questions
  • Reduce response time to 3 minutes (from 15 minutes)
  • Automate 80-90% of inbox management work

Expected Impact: More speed leads to more conversions and more booked calls.

Results Delivered

72-Hour Performance:

  • 342 positive responses
  • Estimated $1.98M in qualified pipeline
  • Tens of thousands of emails sent daily
  • Hundreds of positive replies daily

Conversion Metrics:

  • 68 estimated booked calls (20% conversion from positive reply)
  • 54-55 estimated attended calls (80% show rate)
  • 32-33 estimated SQLs (60% of attended calls)

Why This Works

The Foundation: Strong Offers

The offers deserve significant credit for campaign success:

  • Simple to understand
  • Promise specific results
  • Solve immediate problems
  • Appeal to broad audiences

Result: Can run high volume across large lists without over-niching. Conversion rates are decent (not the best, but decent).

The Execution: System Requirements

The offer alone isn't enough. Success requires:

  1. Reliable infrastructure (deliverability management)
  2. Clean data (quality contact enrichment)
  3. Smart cost optimization (reuse enrichments, optimize AI costs)
  4. Simple, clear offer positioning
  5. Systematic list building (templated processes)
  6. Fast reply management (15-minute response times, moving to 3 minutes)

Key Insight: Scale works when the offer is right AND the execution is flawless. Both elements must be present simultaneously.

7.cardTitle: GEO Firm: 95 Appointments in One Month
7.title: GEO Firm: 95 Sales Appointments in One Month
7.label: Appointments in One Month
7.description:
Campaign Overview

A Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) client engaged Beanstalk Consulting to scale their appointment generation through systematic cold email outbound. Over 6 months, we grew from 1-2 calls booked per day to a peak of 14 calls in a single day, with consistent performance of 10+ daily calls.

Partnership Duration: 6 months

Starting Performance: 1-2 calls per day

Current Performance: 10+ calls per day consistently

Peak Performance: 14 calls in a single day

December Total: 95 calls booked

Tech Stack & Infrastructure

Lead Finding & Enrichment:

  • Clay for lead finding, enrichment, qualification, and all personalization variables
  • AI Ark for contact finding and company discovery
  • Prospeo.io, LeadMagic, and Icypeas for email enrichment waterfall

Messaging & Personalization:

  • Claude for writing personalized email variants and custom variables in every script

Sending Infrastructure:

  • ScaledMail inboxes handling tens of thousands of emails per day with solid reply rates
  • EmailBison for sequencing

Response Management:

  • Clay for categorizing responses and keeping speed-to-lead under 15 minutes

Reporting & Cost Optimization:

  • RevyOps for reporting and central database (saves client thousands of dollars per month)

Strategy & Execution

Approach: Stuck to the basics but ran them relentlessly.

Optimization Process:

  • A/B tested scripts for months
  • Cut what didn't work
  • Doubled down on winners
  • Added AI personalization using custom variables on winning scripts
  • Scaled the winning combinations

Result: Systematic testing and optimization over 6 months grew daily call volume 7x from starting baseline.

Results Delivered

Growth Trajectory:

  • Starting: 1-2 calls per day
  • Current consistent: 10+ calls per day
  • Peak single day: 14 calls booked
  • December total: 95 calls booked

8.cardTitle: M&A Advisory: 1,000 Positive Replies in 3 Months
8.title: M&A Advisory: 1,000 Positive Replies in 3 Months - Beanstalk Case Study
8.label: Positive Replies in 3 Months
8.description:
Campaign Overview

Beanstalk ran parallel cold email campaigns for two buy-side M&A advisory firms simultaneously during Summer 2025, targeting business owners across the United States. Both clients shared the same goal: surface off-market acquisition opportunities by reaching business owners who had never publicly signaled exit intent.

In 3 months, the combined campaigns generated 1,000 positive replies from roughly 80,000 business owners contacted: a 41.9% positive reply rate on cold outreach.

Campaign Duration: 3 months (Summer 2025)

Total Business Owners Contacted: ~79,779

Total Emails Sent: 229,668

Total Replies: 2,388

Positive Replies: 1,000

Positive Reply Rate: 41.9%

Bounce Rate: 1.5%

Industry: M&A Advisory / Business Acquisitions

About the Clients

Client 1: Buy-Side M&A Intermediary

A buy-side intermediary with 20,000+ M&A contacts sourcing off-market deal flow for strategic and financial buyers. Target profile: businesses with $5M+ revenue or $1M+ EBITDA across manufacturing, business services, healthcare, technology, and industrial sectors. Target: owners who had not yet engaged a sell-side banker.

Client 2: Buy-Side Family Office Advisory

A family office actively deploying capital into established businesses with $8M+ EBITDA across manufacturing, industrial/energy, home services, business services, and healthcare. Their outreach led with the family office identity and stated the EBITDA threshold upfront to pre-qualify before anyone replied.

The Challenge

Reaching business owners open to selling is one of the harder cold email problems. Exit intent is something most owners have never articulated externally. No intent data, no trigger event, no public signal.

Both clients needed outreach at volume to generate meaningful deal flow, but scale always introduces deliverability risk. The challenge: build the right lists, write copy that opened conversations without feeling transactional, and keep deliverability clean across hundreds of thousands of sends.

ICP Definition & List Building

For each client, we defined a precise ICP based on their acquisition criteria. Client 1 required a wide net across dozens of industries with exclusion filters for real estate, investment firms, and pre-revenue companies. Client 2 required a tighter industry set with a higher EBITDA floor.

Every contact went through multi-step qualification before entering the campaign:

  • Industry classification and exclusion filtering
  • Revenue and employee count filtering as EBITDA proxies
  • Title targeting to reach owners, CEOs, founders, and principals
  • Email validation to remove invalid and catch-all addresses

The goal was not list size. It was list quality.

Here's how we build AI qualification systems in Clay for large TAMs:

How To 5X Your Leads by Qualifying Companies Before Outreach
https://youtu.be/qlRjnexrKaU

Infrastructure & Deliverability

Sending infrastructure was managed through ScaledMail, using a pool of warmed-up inboxes spread across multiple domains. Distributing send volume across domains protected each one from spam triggers and kept both campaigns in the inbox throughout.

Infrastructure setup:

  • Multiple sending domains with distributed inbox rotation
  • Low daily send volumes per inbox to protect sender reputation
  • Microsoft, Google, and SMTP diversity to avoid provider-level filtering
  • Open tracking intentionally disabled (a standard deliverability optimization)

Across 229,668 emails sent over 3 months, the combined bounce rate landed at 1.5%, well below the 2-3% threshold that typically triggers deliverability problems with major email providers.

Here's how we manage deliverability at scale with ScaledMail:

Cold Email at Scale: Dean Fiacco on Building Scaled Mail for Agencies
https://youtu.be/iz9Bzeiqt_s

Copywriting Strategy

Both campaigns used short, direct sequences designed to feel like a genuine inquiry from a person, not a mass send. The messaging avoided proposal language entirely and instead opened with a simple question about whether the owner had considered an exit.

Client 1 copy was templated around the specific niche of each prospect, making each email feel relevant to their industry even at scale. Subject line variations and body copy spins were tested across sends to avoid pattern fatigue and improve inbox placement. Subject lines used simple, low-friction formats — variations on the company name combined with words like "acquisition," "buyers," or "inquiry."

Client 2 copy introduced the family office directly in the first touch and stated the EBITDA threshold explicitly. Pre-qualifying readers in the first email drove higher-quality responses — owners who replied already understood the offer and were genuinely open to a conversation.

Both campaigns operated on the same core principle: ask a question, don't make a pitch. Business owners are skeptical of cold outreach. Opening with a single honest question about exit interest, then letting the buyer's credentials do the qualifying, lowered the barrier to reply and generated more honest responses.

Step-by-step breakdown of cold email messaging: personalization, subject lines, CTAs, A/B testing, targeting, and follow-up strategy:

I've Sent 30,000,000 Cold Emails, Here's What Works
https://youtu.be/fBiDUJD9EWA

Inbox Management

With nearly 2,400 replies coming in over three months, inbox management was a critical part of the operation. Replies were categorized in real time into positive interest, referrals, out-of-offices, and objections.

Positive replies were flagged and routed to each client immediately for follow-up. At volume, the speed of routing matters — a warm reply from a motivated seller goes cold fast.

Results Delivered

1,000 positive replies from ~80,000 business owners contacted over 3 months.

41.9% of all replies were positive expressions of interest from owners who were genuinely open to an acquisition conversation.

Metric Result
Total business owners contacted ~79,779
Total emails sent 229,668
Total replies 2,388
Positive replies 1,000
Positive reply rate 41.9%
Bounce rate 1.5%
Timeframe 3 months (Summer 2025)

Why It Worked

List quality over volume. Both campaigns prioritized reaching the right contact at the right company over maximizing raw list size. Qualifying by industry, size, and ownership structure before sending meant fewer wasted sends and more relevant conversations.

Copy that asked a question rather than made a pitch. The campaigns opened with a single question about exit interest and let the buyer's credentials do the qualifying. This lowered the barrier to reply and generated more honest, higher-quality responses.

Deliverability infrastructure that protected volume. Running 229,000+ sends over 3 months at a 1.5% bounce rate requires active management of domain health, inbox rotation, and list hygiene. Clean infrastructure kept both campaigns in the inbox throughout the engagement.

Full walkthrough of the complete cold email system: deliverability infrastructure, offer positioning, list building, email writing, follow-up, and CRM sync:

The LAST Video You'll Need To Learn AI Cold Outreach in 2025
https://youtu.be/toG1oklnCAI

9.cardTitle: Options2Exit: 167 Leads & 13-14 Deal Opportunities in 75 Days
9.title: Sell-side M&A advisory Firm - Options2Exit: 167 Leads and 13-14 New Deal Opportunities in the First 75 Days - Beanstalk Case Study
9.label: Leads in 75 Days
9.description:
Client Interview

Watch the full client interview

Campaign Overview

Options2Exit is a sell-side M&A advisory firm for the lower middle market, helping roofing and construction business owners navigate acquisitions and find the right buyer partnerships. They operate out of Delray Beach, Florida, and work with companies doing between $1M and $10M in EBITDA.

Campaign Duration ~2.5 months (as of case study date)
Total Leads Generated 167
New Deal Opportunities 13–14 in first 2 months
Discovery Calls Per Week 5–10 (up from ~1–2 without Beanstalk)
Deals in Active Buyer Conversations 1

Client Background

Options2Exit helps business owners avoid getting taken advantage of by buyers. Their managing director, John Buxton, sold a top-100 roofing company himself, which means when he sits across from a roofing owner, he can talk shop. That founder story is central to their positioning and directly shaped the campaign strategy.

Options2Exit's outbound goal is clear: help owners structure and run a competitive sale process so they don't lose leverage to PE buyers at the 11th hour.

The Challenge

Options2Exit had tried Upwork, offshore agencies, cold calling, and the DIY route through ScaledMail and Clay. Griffin had taught himself DKIM, SPF, and sending limits, but it was eating half his workday. A prior agency made things worse by burning all 7–8 of their domains within a single month, stalling deal flow.

— Griffin Davidson

John, their MD, summed it up:

That's when they reached out to Beanstalk.

Strategy: Campaign Structure

Options2Exit: Targeting roofing owners who had been approached by PE buyers before and experienced late-stage deal changes. Messaging focused on the pain of losing leverage in a buyer-led process.

Targeting & List Building

ICP:

  • Roofing contractors and windows/door installation companies
  • Owner, President, CEO, or Business Owner titles
  • 1–200 employees
  • United States only

List Building Tech Stack:

  • Serper.dev (dedicated workflow) to scrape Google for roofing companies meeting review thresholds, highly effective for finding businesses that don't always surface in traditional B2B databases like Apollo
  • Clay for enrichment, qualification, and contact-level data building
  • AI Ark for contact and company discovery
  • Icypeas, Leadmagic, Prospeo for email enrichment
  • BounceBan for email validation

The client also provided historical lists: previously contacted companies, DNC lists, and contacts from prior outreach, which we integrated to ensure clean suppression and identify warm re-engagement opportunities.

How we think about list building: One of the most expensive mistakes companies make is enriching leads before qualifying them. We run qualification first, enrichment only on what passes. See how this approach cut enrichment costs by 60% for one client

Why we use enrichment waterfalls: No single contact data provider has complete coverage. We layer AI Ark, Clay, Icypeas, Leadmagic, and BounceBan together so that what one misses, the next catches. Coverage jumped from 30–40% to 82% on this type of roofing list. Read more on enrichment waterfalls

Infrastructure & Deliverability

After a prior agency burned every domain the client owned in under a month, deliverability management was the top priority from day one.

Setup:

  • Sending domains set up as derivatives of options2exit.com, with primary domains fully protected at all times
  • Full DNS configuration: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC on all sending domains before a single email was sent
  • EmailBison used as the sequencing platform
  • Gradual warmup protocol before campaigns went live
  • Inbox placement tested regularly across all sending accounts
  • Underperforming inboxes replaced proactively rather than reactively
  • 2:1 warmup-to-cold-send ratio maintained throughout

The client's prior experience with domain burning meant they were acutely aware of how fragile sending infrastructure can be. No burned domains. No deliverability crises.

Why infrastructure matters more than ever in 2026: Pattern detection is getting smarter, safe volume limits are shrinking, and the cost of torching your primary domain is higher than most people realize. Watch Dean break down the cold email infrastructure changes coming in 2026

19 actionable strategies for email deliverability: A full breakdown of everything from DKIM/SPF setup to inbox rotation to why plain text outperforms designed emails. Watch here

Messaging Strategy

The campaign ran multiple angle variants simultaneously, tested before scaling the winners.

Options2Exit angles tested:

  • Prior buyer outreach and late-stage deal fatigue, targeting owners who had gone through a deal process and been burned by last-minute valuation changes
  • Comparison shopping and loss of control, targeting owners watching PE firms acquire competitors around them
  • Market awareness and process education with a consultative, no-pressure framing

Copy principles across all campaigns:

  • Short, direct messaging that respected the prospect's time (50–90 words)
  • Pain-first positioning built around what owners had already experienced (PE burnout, late deal changes, lost leverage) rather than vague promises
  • Low-friction CTAs: exploratory conversations and market context, not hard pitches
  • No em dashes, no corporate language, no AI-sounding sentences

On AI personalization: We don't use AI personalization for the sake of it. When it's tied directly to the offer and relevant to the prospect's specific situation, it moves the needle. When it's not, a tight broad email usually outperforms. Read Leo's take on when AI personalization is actually worth it

Event-Based Campaign: IRE Las Vegas

When Options2Exit exhibited at the International Roofing Expo (IRE) in Las Vegas, a dedicated campaign was built targeting the IRE attendee list. The campaign invited prospects to visit their booth and participate in a live podcast recorded at the show, a unique angle that stood out against the typical trade show follow-up noise.

Griffin and John were both on the floor throughout the show, giving the campaign a personal, accessible feel: real names, real people, a real reason to stop by.

Results Delivered

In approximately 2.5 months from campaign launch:

Lead Generation:

  • 167 qualified leads generated
  • 13–14 new deal opportunities created in the first two months
  • 1 opportunity already in active buyer conversation

Call Volume:

  • 5–10 new discovery calls per week with businesses that had never spoken to Options2Exit before
  • Up from an estimated 1–2 calls per week without Beanstalk's outbound system

Business Impact:

Options2Exit went from a stalled deal pipeline (caused by a prior agency burning all their domains) to 13–14 active opportunities across their funnel in under 60 days. For a firm where one closed transaction generates significant advisory fees, consistent deal flow at this level represents substantial pipeline value. Their longer sales cycle and niche qualification criteria mean the conversion window is extended, but the pipeline is real and being actively worked.

As Dean noted during the interview:
Client Testimonial

— Griffin Davidson, M&A Advisor, Options2Exit

What Griffin Said About Working With Beanstalk

During a recorded client interview, Griffin walked through his full journey with outbound before and after Beanstalk:
